fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer occupational therapist license to louisiana, occupational therapist compact louisiana, louisiana occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners — OT
board.mailing_address: 1520 O'Neal Lane, Building 2, Suite B, Baton Rouge, LA 70816

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Louisiana.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Accela Account
steps.description: Access the LSBOTE Accela Citizen Access Portal and click "Register for an Account" to establish your user profile, then verify your email address to activate your account.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Log in to your Accela account and select "Apply for a License" or navigate to the 'OT/OTA Application' section to begin the online application form for initial licensure or endorsement.

steps.title: Complete Application Sections
steps.description: Accurately fill out all required sections of the online application, including personal details, educational history, examination information (NBCOT), criminal history, and any prior professional licensure.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documents
steps.description: Electronically submit all necessary documents through the Accela portal. This typically includes official transcripts from your OT program, NBCOT score reports, verification of licensure from any other states where you hold or have held a license, and any other requested forms.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $100.00 (for initial or endorsement) directly through the Accela system using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Application
steps.description: Review your complete application for accuracy, electronically submit it, and regularly check your Accela account for updates on the status of your application and any requests for additional information from LSBOTE staff.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Louisiana has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Louisiana
hero.dek: Apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Louisiana through the Louisiana State Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners (LSBOTE) Accela Citizen Access Portal. This online system handles all new applications, including endorsement.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the LSBOTE Accela Citizen Access Portal.
2. Click on "Register for an Account" and follow the prompts to create your user profile, agreeing to the terms of use.
3. Verify your email address by clicking the link sent to your registered email to complete the account activation.
